prs.title: Yaila semantic search
prs.body:
- Integrated Endee-style semantic search into YAILA backend
- Added new public APIs:
  - /api/semantic/index
  - /api/semantic/search
- Implemented fallback in-memory vector search (works without Endee running)
- Kept all existing YAILA functionality unchanged

Demo:
- Backend runs on port 5001
- Semantic indexing + search works via curl

This is a backend-first demo focused on reliability and system design.

prs.title: Store package version release date in DB (fixes #1295)
prs.body:
This PR adds support for persisting package version release dates.

Changes
- Added nullable/indexed `release_date` to:
- `Package`
- `PackageV2`
- Added migration: `0117_package_release_date.py`
- Updated `ValidVersionImprover.get_package_versions()` to save fetched release dates to matching package versions.
- Added a regression test to confirm release dates are stored for both `Package` and `PackageV2`.

Closes #1295

prs.title: Fix EPSS tab to show latest score by published date
prs.body:
This PR fixes an issue where the EPSS tab could show an older EPSS value even when a newer one existed.

What was happening was that in the EPSS tab flow, we were picking EPSS severity with `.first()`.  Because the default ordering for severities is not based on recency, that could return an older EPSS record.

What changed
- Added a helper to consistently select the latest EPSS severity by:
  - `published_at` descending
  - `id` descending as a fallback
- Updated both places that build EPSS tab data:
  - vulnerability details view
  - advisory details view

Test coverage
Added a regression test that creates two EPSS records (older + newer) for the same vulnerability and verifies the EPSS tab context uses the newer one.

Manual check
I also verified this locally using a demo vulnerability with:
- old EPSS: `0.00045`
- newer EPSS: `0.21233`

After this change, the EPSS tab correctly shows the newer score, percentile, and published date.

Closes #1975

prs.title: this is all the changes i made
docs: add Tailwind example for overriding Shiki background/foreground (fixes #983)

labels.name: seems-ai-generated
labels.description: We label this as a rejection to PR that seems to be AI generated. Please do not do that.

prs.body:
Fixes #983

This PR adds a short how-to showing two simple approaches to map Tailwind colors to the CSS variables used by Shiki themes:

- Global :root mapping (simple)
- Scoped mapping with Tailwind `@layer base` (recommended for componentized sites)

Included:
- Example Tailwind CSS snippets
- Example CSS with CSS variables that Shiki's `createCssVariablesTheme` can read
- Usage notes and verification steps

If maintainers prefer this content be placed into an existing page (e.g., theme-colors), I can move it or update as requested.
